body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's primitive is parser/tailer robustness, not an agent feature — the scenario meaning states plainly 'No special agent capability required.' From aider's side there is nothing to 'support': aider appends assistant prose and `> …` status blockquotes to `.aider.chat.history.md` (markdown, written per-project in CWD), terminating each line with `\n`. Whatever length a line happens to be, aider just flushes it. So agent_supports is trivially yes. (In practice aider's markdown wraps and tool output lands as multi-line blockquotes, so aider does not naturally emit a single multi-MB line — the oversized line is a synthetic stress, see caveat.)

The spec's only observable is the state arc `ready → working → ready` with no parser stall leaving the session stuck in `working`.

**daemon_capability: full.** The oversized-line protection is SHARED tailer infrastructure, not adapter-specific. In `core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go` the read loop (`TailAndProcess`) builds a `bufio.NewReaderSize(file, 64*1024)` (line 482) and calls `readLineCapped(reader, maxTranscriptLineSize)` at line 488 — BEFORE the `isRawLine` branch at line 515 that routes the trimmed line into aider's `ParseLineRaw`. So aider's `RawLineParser` path is gated by exactly the same cap as the JSONL path (NOT `bufio.Scanner`, whose old ~2 MB cap would have wedged). `maxTranscriptLineSize` is 64 MB (line 22); lines beyond that are logged ('skipping oversized line at offset N', line 496) and the offset is advanced past them so processing continues, and an `errPartialAtEOF` path (line 489) re-reads a partially-written giant line once more bytes arrive. This resolves the matrix's prior `daemon_capability: unknown` / 'RawLineParser; behavior on very long lines unvalidated' note — the RawLineParser route is protected identically to JSONL, so a trace exists in the watched Source AND the parser handles it today: `full`, not `bug`/`incapable`.

For aider specifically the canonical recipe's ~30 KB line (5000 words) is far under the 64 MB cap, so the line is read whole and handed to `ParseLineRaw`, which buffers a giant prose line into `assistantBuffer` and resolves the turn via the usual `> Tokens:` / IdleFlush path — `ready → working → ready` completes. Even a hand-crafted >64 MB line only triggers the skip-and-advance branch, which still leaves the arc intact. `core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go` pins the regression (oversized line does not block the tailer; subsequent lines still parse), and `TestReadLineCapped_OversizedLineSkipped` / `_PartialAtEOF` / `_PartialOversizedAtEOF` exercise the cap, partial-at-EOF, and oversized-partial-at-EOF paths directly.

**driver_capability: ready.** The recipe drives a single `send` (the long-sentence prompt), `wait_turn` (block on the next `> Tokens:` close marker), and a trailing `sleep` for the idle-flush settle — all three are implemented in `scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`. Eliciting a large single line needs only `send`, which is present; no gapped primitive (restart/reset_session/resume/sigkill/keys/…) is on the recipe path.

Net: any line under 64 MB is parsed normally, any line over it is skipped with a log line — either way the turn arc completes and irrlicht observes the full `ready → working → ready` topology with a driver that has every step the recipe needs.
caveats: Aider does not naturally emit a single oversized line (markdown prose wraps; tool output is multi-line `>` blockquotes), so the recipe must synthesize the oversized line — e.g. prompt aider for one very long unwrapped sentence, or append hand-crafted bytes into the live `.aider.chat.history.md`. The shared tailer path is exercised either way.

Lines above the 64 MB cap are dropped from the parser's point of view (the offset still advances, but the line's content is lost). The spec asserts only the state arc, not the content, so this is fine for the cell; it is operator-visible in the daemon log only.

Aider has no in-band end-of-turn marker, so the working→ready transition for an oversized-line turn still relies on the 1500ms IdleFlush (effectively ~5s with the SessionDetector refresh tick); the arc completes, just not instantaneously after the line lands.

sources.note: TailAndProcess builds bufio.NewReaderSize(file, 64KB) (line 482) and calls readLineCapped(reader, maxTranscriptLineSize) at line 488 BEFORE the isRawLine branch at line 515 — aider's RawLineParser path is gated by the same oversized-line cap as JSONL. maxTranscriptLineSize=64MB (line 22); oversized lines logged ('skipping oversized line', line 496) and skipped past, not stalled. Anchors daemon_capability: full (parser handles the trace today).

Regression test for #270 — oversized line does not block the tailer; subsequent lines still parse. TestReadLineCapped_OversizedLineSkipped / _PartialAtEOF / _PartialOversizedAtEOF exercise the cap directly.
Aider uses FilesUnderCWD + RawLineParser (markdown .aider.chat.history.md), so it routes through the shared tailer read loop just like every other adapter.

ParseLineRaw buffers a whole line into assistantBuffer; turn resolves via `> Tokens:` assistant_message + IdleFlush turn_done, driving working→ready. No per-line size handling — that lives in the tailer.
Oversized line wedge fixed by switching the tailer from bufio.Scanner to bufio.Reader + readLineCapped — adapter-agnostic, so it covers aider's RawLineParser path.
